name: Tour of the Ayous Lakes
name_original: Grande randonnée des lacs de Roumassot, Bersau et Castérau
description: This difficult 9.8-mile hike in the Pyrenees offers stunning views of Pic du Midi d'Ossau and passes several beautiful glacial lakes.
tips: This valley follows the course of the Gave de Bious and offers a superb deep and clear panorama. A green expanse in contrast with the passes, peaks and rocky massifs that rise to the horizon. The path is gentle, flat or very slightly sloping, which makes the valley all the more peaceful.

Lake Roumassot is the lowest of the Ayous lakes. This natural lake of glacial origin is located at an altitude of 1,845 meters and serves as a summer pasture for the herds of the Ossau valley. The presence of horses and cows adds an authentic touch to the painting.

The Ayous lakes form a group of six lakes: Castérau, Miey, Gentau, Bersau, Paradis and Roumassot.

Arranged in stairs, they form a magnificent amphitheater to land and admire the Pic du Midi d'Ossau.

Camping is authorized around Lake Gentau, at the foot of the Ayous refuge.

The Ayous refuge is open from the end of May to the beginning of October. For the precise dates, which change from one year to the next, you can go to the refuge website: https://www.refuge-ayous.fr/. You will find lots of other information there and can book your night. Also note that it is possible to bivouac around the refuge.

Lake Bersau is located 2,080 meters above sea level, at the foot of Pic Casterau. It is one of the six lakes of Ayous which mark out the paths of the Pyrenean Haute Route and the GR 10, in this sector.

Lake Castérau is located on the edge of the road to Saint-Jacques-de-Compostelle. It is one of the six lakes of Ayous. Located at the foot of the Castérau peak (2,227 meters above sea level), the lake is also on a beautiful open platform offering a breathtaking view of the Midi d'Ossau peak.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Great hike of the lakes of Roumassot, Bersau and Castérau?
faqs.answer: This challenging hike typically takes around 5 hours and 30 minutes to complete. However, given its difficulty and significant elevation gain, it's wise to allow extra time for breaks, enjoying the views, and navigating the varied terrain.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike?
faqs.answer: This route is considered difficult, demanding very good fitness and sure-footedness. It involves a significant elevation gain of about 775 meters over approximately 15.8 kilometers, with varied and sometimes steep terrain. It corresponds to a SAC (Swiss Alpine Club) rating of 4-6.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll encounter a mix of unpaved paths, natural surfaces, and some short asphalt sections. Expect steep and winding parts, especially through forests and along a picturesque waterfall. The descent from Lac Castérau is particularly noted for being steep, winding, and rocky. At higher altitudes, such as around Lac Bersau, you might find snow and melting ice even in late spring.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The hike offers a superb panorama of the Ossau Valley and consistent, incredible views of the majestic Pic du Midi d'Ossau. You'll pass several stunning glacial lakes, including Lac Roumassot, Lac Miey, Lac Gentau, Lac Bersau, and Lac Castérau, which are arranged like stairs. Lac Gentau is considered one of the most beautiful, and Lac Castérau offers striking reflections of the Pic du Midi d'Ossau.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The ideal time to hike this trail is generally from late spring to early autumn. The Refuge d'Ayous is typically open from late May to early October, which aligns with the best conditions. Be aware that snow and melting ice can still be present at higher altitudes, particularly around Lac Bersau, even in late spring.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, portions of this route align with the GR10, a renowned long-distance hiking path that traverses the French Pyrenees. It also passes through sections of the Voie d'Ossau, La traversée des Pyrénées - sec.7, and the Sentier du Tour des Lacs d'Ayous PNP.

faqs.question: Is the Great hike of the lakes of Roumassot, Bersau and Castérau dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this trail is located within the Pyrenees National Park, there are generally restrictions on dogs. Dogs are typically not allowed, even on a leash, in the central zone of the National Park to protect wildlife. Please check the latest regulations from the Pyrenees National Park authority before planning your visit with a dog.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or water sources available on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, near Lac Gentau, you'll find the Refuge d'Ayous. This refuge provides water and restroom facilities and is usually open from late May to early October. It's always recommended to carry sufficient water for your hike, especially outside of the refuge's operating season.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the Pyrenees National Park. While there isn't typically an entrance fee for hiking in the park, certain regulations apply, especially regarding camping, fires, and dogs. It's always a good idea to check the official Pyrenees National Park website for any specific rules or temporary restrictions before your visit.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The most common starting point for hikes in this area, including routes to the Ayous lakes, is often from the Bious-Artigues car park. From there, various paths lead into the network of trails. It's advisable to arrive early, especially during peak season, as parking can fill up quickly.

faqs.question: Is it possible to camp along the trail?
faqs.answer: Wild camping is generally regulated within the Pyrenees National Park. However, near Lac Gentau, there is a wide esplanade where bivouacking is permitted under specific conditions, usually between 7 PM and 9 AM, and at least an hour's walk from road access. Always respect park rules and leave no trace.

waypoints.name: Lac Gentau and View of Pic du Midi d'Ossau
